_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Develop enterprise architecture or system components required to meet user needs.
reference_document_element_description: Design and document the enterprise ML platform architecture, including training infrastructure, feature store design, model registry, serving layer, and monitoring stack.
comments: DCWF task superset of WIDAI AI domain. AI-T-011 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-T-001 (DA, cosine: 0.583), DA-S-001 (DA, cosine: 0.561).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.6/10 (raw 0.556). Shared vocabulary: [architecture, enterprise]. DCWF-only terms [components, develop, meet, needs, required, system] vs WIDAI-only terms [design, document, feature, infrastructure, layer, ml] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.868 (P=0.883, R=0.854). P exceeds R by 0.029 — the DCWF element's 11 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 21 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). "Develop enterprise architecture or system components required to meet user needs" and "Design and document the enterprise ML platform architecture, including training" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.611 > STS 0.556 (gap +0.054). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [architecture, enterprise] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.1%. Shared tokens: [architecture, enterprise]. DCWF-only: [components, develop, meet, needs, required, system]. WIDAI-only: [design, document, feature, infrastructure, layer, ml].
